evidence_quotes.quote: In the videogame industry it has become a common practice to make any game require an internet connection,
I have personally been a victim of completely loosing access to a game I bought, when ubisoft shutdown The Crew 1.
a game from 2015 got removed and consumers got their access revoked with no reimbursement or refunds.

paragraphs.text: I wanna start out by saying its great to see the EU take a bigger stand against some of the practices made by companies all around the world when it comes to consumer rights! As a gamer it has only become more clear that we need more laws surrounding this area! In the videogame industry it has become a common practice to make any game require an internet connection, this means that if and when the company don't want to support the game anymore they can essentially make it completely unplayable with no concerns for the consumer having spent real life money on the game or microtransactions in the game. Right now the stop killing games movement is going on and I would absolutely love to see these two initiatives work together for more consumer rights and protection in the future! I have personally been a victim of completely loosing access to a game I bought, when ubisoft shutdown The Crew 1. I think its unfair that these video game companies can almost design their games to be obsolete and not give us (the consumers) any chance of being able to still play the game. We can play games from the 90´s, but a game from 2015 got removed and consumers got their access revoked with no reimbursement or refunds. Again, just wanna say how happy I am more is being done about this!
